decisions.decision: Target OpenAPI 3.0.3 for maximum tooling compatibility (Swagger UI, code generators, linters)
alternatives_rejected: OpenAPI 3.1.0 — better JSON Schema alignment but narrower tooling support; project schemas are simple enough that 3.0.3 handles them well

decisions.decision: Exclude WebSocket endpoints from the OpenAPI spec (REST-only focus)
alternatives_rejected: Include WS via AsyncAPI or x-extensions — adds significant complexity for 3 endpoints that OpenAPI 3.0 cannot natively represent
Exclude legacy endpoints — monitoring tools (probe scripts) depend on /healthz per t586 docs

decisions.decision: Document auth as required on protected routes (production behavior) with a description note about the BAYES_REQUIRE_AGENT_ID env var toggle
alternatives_rejected: Document both modes with conditional security — adds complexity for a dev-only toggle
Document as optional — misleads about production expectations

decisions.decision: Use contract freeze docs as primary schema source for frozen endpoints, server.py for undocumented endpoints, cross-referencing both
alternatives_rejected: Server.py only — misses agreed contract guarantees
Contract docs only — may miss endpoints without contract docs

decisions.decision: Output as a single docs/openapi.yaml file with tag-based grouping (Health, Markets, Orders, Accounts)
alternatives_rejected: Multiple spec files per domain — unnecessary complexity for ~21 endpoints in one server

steps.task: Create OpenAPI spec skeleton: info block (title, description, version, contact), servers array, tags (Health, Markets, Orders, Accounts), and securitySchemes (X-Bayes-Agent-Id apiKey header)

Define shared component schemas: ErrorResponse envelope ({error: {code, message, details}, meta: {timestamp}}), MetaObject, standard error codes (400/401/404/405/422/429/500), and pagination parameters

Document Health endpoints: GET / (service index), GET /health + /healthz (legacy health with {service, status, timestamp}), GET /v1/health (component health with db/inference/auth breakdown). Define HealthResponse and V1HealthResponse schemas

Document Market read endpoints: GET /v1/markets (list with status/sort/q query params), GET /v1/markets/{id} (detail), GET /v1/markets/{id}/meta (preview), GET /v1/markets/{id}/engine-stats, GET /v1/markets/{id}/analytics, GET /v1/markets/{id}/events (paginated), GET /v1/markets/{id}/comments (paginated). Define Market, MarketDetail, MarketPreview, EngineStats, MarketEvent, Comment schemas

Document Market write endpoints: POST /v1/markets (create with title/description/outcomes/expires_at/liquidity/maxPositionSize), POST /v1/markets/{id}/comments (post comment), POST /v1/markets/{id}/resolve (resolve market). Define request body schemas and CommandAccepted/CommandRejected response schemas per t544 contract

Document Market order endpoints: POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit (per t1737 contract), POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/event-trade. Define ProbabilityEditRequest/Response and EventTradeRequest/Response schemas cross-referencing t586 and t1737 contract freeze docs

Document Account endpoints: GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/exposure, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/positions, GET /v1/accounts/{id}/pnl, GET /v1/markets/{market_id}/accounts/{account_id}/pnl. Define AccountRisk, Exposure, Position, PnL schemas
Validate the complete spec: check YAML syntax, verify all $ref pointers resolve, ensure all endpoints have responses defined, and confirm schema consistency with contract freeze docs

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Although estimated_total_lines exceeds 500, this is a single output file (docs/openapi.yaml) where all sections share component schemas, security definitions, and must be internally consistent. Spawning children to write to the same YAML file would create merge conflicts and consistency issues. There are 0 high-risk steps (3 medium at most), and while steps 3-7 are logically parallel, they cannot be independently written to the same file. Both prior phases (questions, research) independently concluded PROCEED_ALONE for the same reason.
